California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
December 3, 1987 
Mr. Phil Anderson 
P.O. Box 714 
Walnut, CA 
91788-0714 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I 87-276 
Dear Mr. Anderson: 
The Commission is in receipt of your letter dated 
October 26, 1987, and a copy of a mass mailing which was sent to 
the voters on October 26, 1987, supporting your candidacy in the 
November 3, 1987 election.lI 
Government Code section 84305~ was amended effective 
October 1, 1978, by chapter 1408 of the statutes of 1978, to 
delete the requirement that a copy of all mass mailings be sent 
to the Fair Political Practices Commission. 
The 1987 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure 
Provisions of the Political Reform Act," Part IX (copy enclosed), 
provides recordkeeping guidelines for candidates and committee 
treasurers. 
Subsection 3 on page 41 of the manual sets out the 
types of documents which require retention. Here it states that 
"A copy of each mass mailing made by a candidate or committee 
should also be kept." 
You must retain a copy of all mass 
mailings for a period of four years. 
(Section 84104; Regulation 
18401 copy enclosed.) 
J 
lIYour letter states only a general question. 
Therefore, 
consider it to 
a request for informal assistanc,e pursuant 
18329(c) (copy enclosed). 
Informal asistance 
the 
with the immunity provided by an 
written advice. 
(Section 84113; Regulation 
Government Code 
ion 
at 2 
section 18000, et seq. 
All 
to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the 
Administrative Code.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions.

PHIL "ANDY" ANDERSON 18063 Jeannie Dr .• la Puente. CA 91744 (818) 964-5252 
Dear Voters, 
Someone once said, "Campaign Promises are made to be broken." 
I don't happen to share that point of view, and I don't think you do either. 
You have elected me to serve our district since 1969, and once again I am 
asking you for your vote on NOVEMBER 3, 1987. 
My record has been established. It is a Proven record of performance. 
Be sure to vote, and take a friend. Every vote counts. 
Thank you in advance 
A copy of my official candidate statement is enclosed in case you lost or 
misplaced yours. 
let's Keep Rowland Unified 
Vote On November 3rd, 1987 


Phil "Andy" Anderson 18063 Jeannie Dr. La Puente Ca 91744 (818) 964-5252 
Reprinted for you is a copy of my Candidate Statement - in case you lost or 
misplaced your Sample Ballot. 
STATEMENT OF: PHIL "ANDY" ANDERSON 
CANDIDATE FOR GOVERNING BOARD MEMBER 
ROWLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT 
You the voters have honored me by re-electing me to office on the Rowland 
Unified School Board since 1969. I am pleased to report to you that our school 
district is in excellent condition. Test scores are at the highest level ever. Three 
elementary schools are identified as distinguished California schools. All three 
high schools are big winners in the Cash for CAP program enabling them to 
use the money for staff training, a library addition and other program improve-
ments. The District offers programs ranging from Kindergarten (PREPPIE) to 
high school that serve the unique needs of our students. Adult education classes 
are offering opportunities for adults to re-train for new jobs, obtain high school 
diplomas, and learn our language and study about our political system to 
become citizens. As your elected representative, I have demanded efficient 
management and have fought for student safety through substance abuse 
training, bussing, and strong diSCipline policies. 
Your vote will enable me to continue representing both parent and non-parent 
taxpayers while striving for maximum use of our tax dollar. Youth is our most 
important asset and my experience as a board member will enable me to 
continue the development of Rowland's total educational system. 
VOTE FOR ANDY 
NOVEMBER 3, 1987 
ROWLAND UNIRED SCHOOL DISTRICT 
LET'S KEEP ROWlAND UNIFIED - VOTE ON NOVEMBER 3rd, 1987
